masterFinding.title: Master finding
masterFinding.description: Article framework - ICS must be interpreted together with FLOOR.
masterFinding.captureThreshold: No model exceeds the idiomaticity capture threshold (ICS ≥ {{capture}}); partial threshold ICS ≥ {{partial}}.
masterFinding.ordinaryControl: The ordinary-perturbation control (Experiment 4) shows this failure is specific to idiomaticity, not a general measurement artifact: OCG (idiom) < {{baseline}} for all models.

ocg.title: Experiment 4 - Ordinary-perturbation calibration (OCG)
ocg.description: OCG (idiom) = idiomatic(synonym−random) / ordinary(synonym−random). 1 = as good as ordinary; < 1 = weaker idiomatic discrimination. Compositional ≈ 1 expected.

scatter.title: Indicator relationship
scatter.description: Per-measurement distribution (each point is a model/language/context), colored by family

parity.title: English parity
parity.subtitle: How close each Turkish item is to its English NCIMP counterpart. Each item targets four probe families plus a human gold score; the bars and the to-do list show what is still missing.

legend.title: What each dimension means

dimensionHelp.P_Syn: Sentence where the whole expression is swapped for a synonym.
dimensionHelp.P_Comp: Sentence where one component word is dropped/replaced.
dimensionHelp.P_WordsSyn: Sentence where each word is swapped for a word-level synonym.
dimensionHelp.P_Rand: Control sentence with a random replacement.
dimensionHelp.gold: Aggregated human compositionality score from the annotation pilot.

table.title: Per-item closeness

annotation.title: Annotation
annotation.subtitle: Type tasks score an MWE with three natural contexts; token tasks score a single context. Gold data requires eight independent annotations and curator acceptance per item.
annotation.subtitleAnnotator: Open each of your tasks below and score the ready-made sentences.

enableFirestore.title: Enable Firestore project
enableFirestore.description: Local seed is currently shown; load data into Firestore once to write tasks and responses.

campaign.title: Launch campaign
campaign.description: Pre-labels are for guideline development and do not count as gold. The official pilot requires at least 8 distinct annotators.
campaign.prelabelExplain: Pre-labeling: you alone label every item to draft and test the guidelines. This is practice — it does not count as gold data. Use this to try the workflow first.
campaign.pilotExplain: Pilot: the official run. At least 8 different people independently label the same items so we can measure how much they agree. Tick the annotators below, then start.
campaign.launchedHint: This campaign is already launched. Your tasks are in the “My tasks” section below — click a task to start scoring.
campaign.annotatorsAdminOnly: Registered annotators are listed only on admin accounts.

assignments.title: My tasks
assignments.description: {{count}} tasks · only records assigned to you
assignments.empty: No tasks assigned to you yet. They appear here when ready; click one to score it.
assignments.emptyAdmin: No tasks assigned to you yet. They appear here when ready; click one to score it.
assignments.formHint: Pick a task on the left, then score the ready-made sentences (0–5) and write a paraphrase. You don't write sentences — they come with the task.

form.contextsLabel: Sentences to score (the expression is highlighted):
form.paraphrasePlaceholder: Explain the expression in other words in this context…
form.submitNote: Submitted annotations cannot be edited later; this preserves independent judgment.
form.readOnly: This task is {{status}}; the form is read-only.

help.title: How to score (quick guide)
help.task: You see the highlighted expression used in real sentences. Judge how much its overall meaning is built from the literal (dictionary) meanings of its individual words, then give the scores below and rewrite the meaning in your own words. The sentences are given — you do not write them.
help.scaleHigh: 5 = fully literal: the meaning is just the words combined. Example: “red car” = a car that is red.
help.scaleMid: 2–3 = partly figurative: one word is literal, the other is not. Example: “black list” — it is a real list, but “black” is not about the colour.
help.scaleLow: 0 = fully idiomatic: the meaning has nothing to do with the literal words. Example: “kick the bucket” = to die.

hints.modifier: Does “{{word}}” keep its literal meaning here? 0 = no, 5 = fully literal.
hints.head: Does “{{word}}” keep its literal meaning here? 0 = no, 5 = fully literal.
hints.paraphrase: Rewrite what the expression means in THIS sentence, in plain words.
hints.paraphraseExample: Example — sentence: “He was put on the black list.” → paraphrase: “the group of people who are banned or not trusted.”

review.title: Review (approve submitted ratings)
review.description: {{count}} ratings awaiting approval. Approved ones feed the shared reliable result (gold).

aggregation.title: Combine results and decide
aggregation.description: Combines approved independent ratings into agreement, a confidence interval, and the final shared decision (gold).
aggregation.aggregateNowSuccess: Approved ratings combined in the browser.

examples.subtitle: 3 natural + 2 neutral contexts per MWE. Rule: at least one natural example must be corpus-sourced. Missing corpus example:

footer.title: NCIMP Idiomaticity Research Atlas
footer.description: Combines the published NCIMP study, a 2024-2026 model re-examination, and Turkish dataset extension on one traceable research surface.

hero.title: Do models read idioms by their
hero.description: With NCIMP minimal pairs we measure whether a model truly represents “grey matter” as “brain”. We rebuild the published English-Portuguese study, extend it to 2026 LLMs, calibrate with ordinary controls, and prepare the Turkish counterpart.

demo.sentence: These youngsters use their grey matter when the presentation is right.
demo.alert: The model sees the literal component matter (0.78) as closest instead of the correct meaning brain (0.55).

P_Syn.label: Holistic synonym
P_Syn.note: Preserves the true meaning; expected to score highest.

P_Comp.label: Single component
P_Comp.note: Loses the idiom's meaning; incorrectly highest here.

P_WordsSyn.label: Word-by-word synonym
P_WordsSyn.note: Keeps the words but not the holistic meaning.

P_Rand.label: Frequency-matched random
P_Rand.note: Unrelated lower bound; expected to score lowest.

S.name: Similarity
S.explanation: Cosine closeness between the original representation and the perturbed representation.

A.name: Affinity
A.explanation: States which of two probes the model sees as closer to the target.

SR.name: Scaled Similarity
SR.explanation: Removes the model's random-similarity baseline to reveal meaningful signal.

ISC.name: Idiomatic Synonym Capture
ISC.explanation: Measures how well idioms' true holistic synonyms are recovered.

IG.name: Idiomaticity Gap
IG.explanation: Capture gap between compositional expressions and idioms.

LOD.name: Lexical-Overlap Dominance
LOD.explanation: Tests whether word-by-word literal alternatives beat the true meaning.
LOD.example: >0: model treats idiom as sum of parts

AID.name: Affinity on Idioms
AID.explanation: Preference for holistic synonym over literal alternative on idioms.

FLOOR.name: Random Similarity Floor
FLOOR.explanation: Shows that even random substitutions look similar and reveals space anisotropy.

RHO.name: Human-score Correlation
RHO.explanation: Measures whether capture depends on human compositionality scores.
RHO.example: High positive ρ: success only on compositionals

ICS.name: Idiomaticity Capture Score
ICS.explanation: Intuitive 0-1 summary of five direction-sensitive diagnostics; not gold truth alone.

OCG.name: Ordinary-Calibrated Gap
OCG.explanation: Divides idiom discrimination by the same model's discrimination on ordinary targets.

01.title: Encoder replication
01.question: Can we reproduce the published NCIMP finding?
01.result: Yes. Every condition ICS<0.55; limited improvement in English, none in Portuguese.

02.title: Modern embedding cohort
02.question: Do new retrieval/embedding models capture holistic meaning?
02.result: Best ICS 0.478. Lexical pressure decreases, threshold still not crossed.

03.title: Decoder-only LLM cohort
03.question: Do scale, generation, or instruction tuning break the bottleneck?
03.result: No. True leader base Gemma 4-12B: ICS 0.525; soft ceiling persists.

04.title: Ordinary perturbation
04.question: Is failure specific to idiomaticity or is measurement generally insensitive?
04.result: Idiom OCG never 1 for any model; best 0.68. Compositional targets hit the ordinary baseline.

sourceTrace.title: Original NCIMP paper
sourceTrace.role: EN/PT data design, human scores, Affinity and Scaled Similarity

sourceTrace.title: NCIMP source code and supplementary material
sourceTrace.role: Original experiment artifacts and reproduction reference

sourceTrace.title: An Idiom Benchmark for Turkish

01.label: 01 / Research question
01.heading: Knowing an idiom is not knowing its parts.
01.paragraph: "Red tape" is bureaucracy, "cash cow" is a steady revenue source, "grey matter" is the brain. Strong surface-word representations are not the same as representing this holistic meaning.

representation.title: Representation question
representation.text: Is the target expression closer in vector space to its true holistic meaning or to literal components?

comparative.title: Comparative measurement
comparative.text: Instead of a single high cosine, we compare meaning-preserving and meaning-breaking controlled substitutions.

crossLingual.title: Cross-lingual test
crossLingual.text: Alongside original English and Portuguese experiments, Turkish is added with the same protocol.

modelAgnostic.title: Model-agnostic probe
modelAgnostic.text: Encoders, sentence embeddings, and decoder LLMs are measured with the same minimal-pair logic.

original.title: Original NCIMP study
original.description: He et al. combined human compositionality scores, natural and neutral contexts, four substitution families, Affinity and Scaled Similarity measures. They built 32,200 sentences for 280 English and 180 Portuguese noun compounds.

newDraft.title: New models + missing control
newDraft.description: We carry the same probe to 20 models: 2024 encoders, modern embedding models, and 0.8B-12B decoder LLMs. Then we add the critical missing comparison: if a model can separate ordinary synonyms from random, how much of that separation does it achieve on idioms?
bullets: ISC, IG, LOD, AID, FLOOR, RHO, ICS

03.label: 03 / Data and minimal-pair design
03.heading: Same sentence, four controlled interventions.
03.paragraph: The sentence skeleton stays fixed; only the target span changes. This makes clear which semantic expectation drives the representation shift.
03.auditNote: The local English snapshot has 281 raw records and 279 scored records; the paper's official scope is 280. "dust storm" and "small fry" score anomalies are not hidden and are noted explicitly in the data explorer.

04.label: 04 / Metric glossary
04.heading: Every score has one question.
04.paragraph: From raw similarity to within-model calibration, all measures are links in the same evidence chain. ICS is a summary; ISC, LOD, AID, FLOOR, and OCG carry the diagnosis.

workedExample.label: Worked example
workedExample.note: The correct meaning is only 15% beyond the random baseline.

05.label: 05 / Four experiments
05.heading: From the original finding to 2026 models, then to a control that tests measurement itself.

representation.title: Why does representation level matter?
representation.text: In full sentences shared words dominate: even random substitution can keep similarity at 0.90-0.99. Primary analysis is therefore the target's contextual span representation, not the whole sentence. For sentence embedding models, isolated phrase is reported as the comparable boundary.

anisotropy.title: Why is anisotropy shown separately?
anisotropy.text: When FLOOR→1 the Scaled Similarity denominator shrinks and small noise grows. Instruction-tuned Gemma 4-12B showing ICS 0.520 is this artifact; FLOOR≈0.95 and ISC≈0 show there is no real capture. ICS is always read together with FLOOR.

bestIcs.label: Best true ICS
bestIcs.note: base Gemma 4-12B; below the 0.55 partial-capture threshold.

bestOcg.label: Best idiom OCG
bestOcg.note: Idiom discrimination is only 68% of the same model's ordinary-target discrimination.

threshold.label: Models above threshold
threshold.note: Soft ceiling holds regardless of family, scale, year, or tuning.

icsChart.title: ICS across 20 models
icsChart.subtitle: English NC level · dashed line partial-capture threshold
icsChart.ariaLabel: ICS values for 20 models; partial-capture threshold 0.55

ocgChart.title: Ordinary-calibrated gap
ocgChart.subtitle: OCG=1 equals the same model's ordinary discrimination performance.
ocgChart.ariaLabel: Idiom and compositional ordinary-calibrated gap values across 20 models

quote.text: New and larger models move in the right direction; yet representing what an idiom means-not just what it says-remains unsolved.
quote.attribution: Combined result: 2024 encoder → 2026 LLM · encoder → embedding → decoder · base → instruct

07.label: 07 / Turkish extension and open data
07.heading: The same experiment, with a defensible gold set in Turkish.
07.paragraph: The Turkish inventory started with 280 two-word candidates, 1,400 base contexts, and 4,200 existing sentences. The target is 19,600 sentences matching the original English design, eight independent annotators per type/token score, four probe families, and 64 ordinary-control records.

openData.title: Readable, immutable, traceable.
openData.description: The public explorer serves EN/PT references, the Turkish working set, and ordinary controls read-only. Visitors receive an anonymous Firebase session; curation write paths are open only to approved roles. When App Check is enabled, Firestore requests are limited to approved site origins.

08.label: 08 / Limitations, quality gates, and source trace
08.heading: We publish the boundary as well as the result.
08.paragraph: This atlas shows completed EN/PT reference, experimental ordinary control, and a not-yet-gold Turkish working snapshot in one place. Being "open" does not mean presenting provisional labels as final truth.

limitations.title: ICS is not gold
limitations.text: ICS is an intuitive composite for ranking models. The real claim rests on reading ISC, LOD, FLOOR, and OCG together.

limitations.title: 64 controls is small
limitations.text: The ordinary-control set is hand-built; some random substitutions are grammatically weak. Semantic, grammar, and frequency review must be completed.

limitations.title: Representation levels differ
limitations.text: Full sentences are misleading because of shared framing. For decoder LLMs contextual span is primary; for sentence embedders isolated phrase is the primary comparison.

limitations.title: Language coverage incomplete
limitations.text: The decoder cohort is currently at English NC level. Portuguese extension and Turkish model runs are deferred until after gold annotation.

limitations.title: Turkish set is provisional
limitations.text: 280 Turkish records are visible with meanings and examples; but 0/280 have gold scores. Provisional I/PC/C classes do not replace human annotation.

limitations.title: Runtime path is recorded
limitations.text: The MLX last-four-layers + final norm recipe is close but not identical to the torch path; model, layer, precision, and runtime are reported with results.

pipeline.title: Turkish release pipeline
pipeline.description: Each gate is versioned and produces an audit record. The public snapshot is separate from the curation database; after publication it cannot be changed by client rules.

sources.title: Primary sources and license trace
sources.description: License status is kept separate from data acceptance; uncertain records are not hidden and carry a review tag.
